data.title: Johnson & johnson outlines $92B 2025 sales guidance amid innovative medicine and medtech growth
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Johnson & Johnson (JNJ) Q1 2025

Management View

  • CEO Joaquin Duato highlighted strong operational sales growth of 4.2% with contributions from both Innovative Medicine and MedTech segments. He emphasized the company's resilience despite the loss of exclusivity for STELARA, attributing performance to their diversified healthcare model.
  • In Innovative Medicine, DARZALEX achieved over 20% growth, with approvals expanding indications in Europe. Other key brands, such as TREMFYA, saw accelerated growth due to new launches in inflammatory bowel disease.
  • MedTech reported 4.1% operational sales growth, driven by cardiovascular units Abiomed and Shockwave. Notable milestones include updates to guidelines for the Impella heart pump and advancements in robotic surgery with new clinical trials for OTTAVA.
  • CFO Joseph Wolk confirmed adjusted net earnings of $6.7 billion and EPS of $2.77 for the quarter. He noted the reversal of a $7 billion talc-related reserve and highlighted the company’s ongoing capital allocation strategy, including a 4.8% dividend increase.
  • EVP John Reed discussed pipeline progress, underscoring the potential of assets like RYBREVANT, TREMFYA, and icotrokinra, along with the anticipated approval of CAPLYTA for major depressive disorder later in the year.

Outlook

  • Full-year 2025 operational sales growth is projected at 3.3%-4.3%, with a midpoint of $92 billion. This includes contributions from the Intra-Cellular acquisition.
  • Adjusted EPS guidance remains $10.50-$10.70, accounting for dilution from the Intra-Cellular acquisition.
  • Management anticipates higher growth in the second half of 2025, supported by newly launched products and pipeline advancements.
  • Biosimilar competition for STELARA is expected to intensify through the year, with incremental revenue contributions from TREMFYA and other immunology assets mitigating the impact.

Financial Results

  • Q1 2025 worldwide sales reached $21.9 billion, with 5.9% growth in the US.
  • Innovative Medicine delivered $13.9 billion in sales, reflecting 4.2% growth despite an 810 basis point headwind from STELARA.
  • MedTech achieved $8 billion in sales, with growth supported by cardiovascular products and surgical vision innovations.
  • Adjusted net earnings were $6.7 billion, representing a 1.9% increase over Q1 2024.
  • Gross margin was impacted by biosimilar competition and product mix changes, including the decline of STELARA.

Q&A

  • Lawrence Biegelsen, Wells Fargo, inquired about tariff impacts. CFO Wolk explained that MedTech tariffs accounted for a significant portion of the $400 million guidance impact but noted ongoing mitigation strategies.
  • Chris Schott, JPMorgan, asked about gross margin declines. Wolk cited STELARA's higher margins and Part D redesign as primary drivers, with management expecting partial margin recovery in subsequent quarters.
  • Asad Haider, Goldman Sachs, questioned STELARA's erosion trajectory. EVP Jennifer Taubert reaffirmed guidance using HUMIRA’s biosimilar erosion pattern as a model.
  • Danielle Antalffy, UBS, explored recession resilience. Management expressed confidence in healthcare demand stability, noting robust procedures and pharmaceutical utilization.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed a cautiously optimistic tone, focusing on tariff impacts, margin recovery, and STELARA's erosion trajectory.
  • Management maintained a confident stance during prepared remarks, emphasizing portfolio resilience and innovation. In Q&A, responses were measured, with clarity on challenges and mitigation strategies.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's tone remained consistent, reinforcing confidence in guidance, while analysts displayed slightly increased scrutiny regarding macroeconomic and competitive pressures.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Sales growth moderated from Q4 2024, reflecting STELARA’s intensifying biosimilar competition.
  • Guidance language remained firm, with incremental clarity on biosimilar impacts and mitigation strategies.
  • Analysts maintained a focus on STELARA erosion and MedTech growth, with added emphasis on tariffs and margin pressures.
  • Management’s confidence in TREMFYA and new MedTech products remained a consistent theme.

Risks and Concerns

  • Biosimilar competition for STELARA remains a significant revenue headwind.
  • Analysts highlighted potential tariff-related cost pressures and their impact on MedTech margins.
  • Management acknowledged competitive dynamics in MedTech, particularly in electrophysiology and orthopedics, alongside challenges in the Chinese market.
  • Mitigation strategies include portfolio diversification, targeted investments in growth areas like TREMFYA and cardiovascular technologies, and operational efficiency improvements.

Final Takeaway

Johnson & Johnson demonstrated resilience in Q1 2025, overcoming significant headwinds from STELARA's loss of exclusivity. The company’s innovation-driven portfolio, especially in oncology and immunology, alongside robust MedTech growth, supports a confident outlook for 2025. Management's emphasis on operational efficiency and targeted investment further underscores their commitment to long-term shareholder value.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Tariffs threaten generic drug production and supply - Fmr. FDA commissioner
data.description:
Dr. Scott Gottlieb, former FDA commissioner and Pfizer board member, warned that proposed tariffs on pharmaceutical imports could destabilize the U.S. generic drug supply.

In an interview with CNBC, he emphasized that while 50% of drugs consumed domestically are manufactured stateside, critical shortages could emerge if tariffs target low-margin generics reliant on Chinese supply chains.

“I fear that if the president places tariffs on those drugs, we’re not just going to see prices increase, we’re going to see companies stop manufacturing them,” he stated, noting that 70% of key chemical ingredients for drugs like antibiotics and heparin originate in China.

Gottlieb highlighted a stark divide between branded and generic drug manufacturers. Branded companies could absorb tariff costs or shift production from Europe to the U.S. due to tax and operational advantages, as seen with recent moves by Eli Lilly (LLY) and Novartis (NVS), he said. Generic manufacturers, however, operate on “very slim margins” and lack flexibility.

He added that the reliance on China extends beyond finished drugs. Over 40% of U.S. heparin supply and nearly all amoxicillin ingredients are sourced there, with intermediate chemicals often processed in India before arriving stateside.

Gottlieb stressed that reshoring this supply chain would require “targeted subsidies” and reduced regulatory hurdles, not tariffs, which risk triggering shortages of “life-saving medicines.”

He urged policymakers to separate trade deficits with Europe from national security concerns tied to China. While branded manufacturers might negotiate a “grand bargain” to return production, generics need a different strategy. “The very thing they’re trying to do is going to be the least amenable to using this tool,” he cautioned, referring to Section 232 tariffs.

Gottlieb’s solution centers on accelerating domestic manufacturing through expedited FDA and environmental approvals, rather than punitive measures.

“It takes three to five years to build a facility,” he noted during the interview, suggesting retrofitting existing sites could offer short-term relief. Without this approach, tariffs risk exacerbating the vulnerabilities they aim to fix, leaving critical generics in short supply.

Here are some of the companies involved: (MRK), (PFE), (ABBV).

data.title: B2Gold to cut 300 jobs, downscale mine operations in Namibia this year
data.description:
B2Gold (NYSE:BTG) -3.5% in Tuesday's trading after saying it plans to cut 300 jobs in Namibia this year as it scales down operations at its Otjikoto mine, extending its phased reduction of operations at the mine that began in Q1 2024.

The Otjikoto mine, which began commercial production in March 2015, produced a record 198,142 oz of gold last year, accounting for nearly 25% of the company's total output, and racked up a record annual profit, helped by record high gold prices.

Open pit mining at the mine is expected to end later this year following the depletion of gold ore, but the company will continue processing stockpiles at least until 2032; the mine's current underground operations are expected to run until 2027, with an extension possible if ongoing exploration successfully uncovers additional mineral deposits.

The Otjikoto mine is expected to produce 165K-185K oz of gold this year.

data.title: Abbott's Q1 earnings in focus as analysts see path to beat amid business strength
data.description:
Abbott Laboratories (NYSE:ABT) is set to report its first-quarter earnings results on Wednesday, April 16th, before the market opens. Wall Street expects modest year-over-year growth, with analysts forecasting EPS of $1.07, representing a 9.2% increase, and revenue of $10.42 billion, up 4.2% from the prior year.

While expectations point to continued growth, sentiment has been slightly cautious heading into the print. Over the past three months, EPS estimates have been revised downward 10 times, compared to just one upward revision. Revenue forecasts have followed a similar trend, with 11 downward revisions and one upward.

Despite the conservative revisions, Abbott has a strong track record of outperforming Street expectations. Over the last two years, the company has beaten EPS and revenue estimates in 88% of the time.

Analyst commentary

RBC Capital Markets maintains an Outperform rating on Abbott and has raised its price target to $140 (from $135), given its "ability to deliver top-tier financial growth in MedTech (high single-digit sales, double-digit EPS growth) aided by multiple growth catalysts."

“Given the less elective nature of its Medical Device portfolio, diabetes business momentum, trends in Diagnostics, recovery in Nutrition, and strength in Established Pharmaceuticals, we expect strong Q1 results positioning it for a beat,” RBC said.

However, the brokerage noted some potential headwind due to global tariff exposure (Abbott operates 89 facilities globally), though operational leverage is expected to offset this.

Stock Performance

Abbott Laboratories (NYSE:ABT) has advanced nearly 12% year-to-date, outperforming XLV’s 0.14% rise.

data.title: CNN plans for 'non-news digital product' this year
data.description:
CNN's top boss, Mark Thompson, has plans to launch a "non-news digital product" this year, according to a Financial Times report from Tuesday.

Thompson told FT the media outlet would debut at least one product this year, which it hopes would generate more than $1B by 2030. He expects to launch one or more products in 2026.

The product "might be heavy on information" and launched initially in the U.S., followed by the rest of the world. The service is expected to have more features and lifestyle content, the report said.

"We’re beginning to build out a suite of digital products, principally with a subscription thesis. I don’t think this stuff is easy. Our entire industry is going through a revolution. The landscape is continuing to go through the stresses and strains of an enormous disruption of audiences and of the media generally," Thompson told FT from CNN’s New York office.

"We want to find a range of ways of meeting different audience needs and demands. And I’m a firm believer that portfolios have a number of quite powerful advantages, that you can maximize the way you meet people’s needs in a more precise way," Thompson told FT.

"We’ve got some quite interesting ideas about things which are not conventional television, but which will be part of the offer as well as the core news," he added.

data.title: SA analyst rating changes: ORCL, NVDA, RDDT, PLCE, DG, POWL, CNDT
data.description:
Here are the latest Seeking Alpha analyst rating changes:

Upgrades:

Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA). Hold to buy by Johnny Zhang. "NVDA's robust growth momentum and strong balance sheet help the company navigate a potential recession scenario. Its supply constraints for AI chips provide a cushion against demand weakness during recession."

Oracle (NYSE:ORCL). Hold to buy by Juxtaposed Ideas. "With ORCL stock now trading (somewhat) nearer to historical trends and its peers, its high-growth investment thesis is more compelling as the management guides double-digits cloud growth opportunities."

Reddit (NYSE:RDDT). Hold to buy by Julian Lin. "Despite tariff uncertainties, RDDT's low ARPU compared to peers suggests sustained 20% top-line growth over the next decade."

Children's Place (NASDAQ:PLCE). Sell to hold by Jeffrey Fischer. "Given the new strategic direction and improved profitability, I upgrade PLCE to a Hold, though the tough competitive landscape for Children's Place remains."

Powell Industries (NASDAQ:POWL). Hold to buy by Juxtaposed Ideas. "Upgraded to a Buy due to dual return prospects from capital appreciation and dividend income, with POWL now trading well below its fair value estimates."

Downgrades: 

Dollar General (NYSE:DG). Hold to sell by Eleceed Capital. "I downgrade Dollar General from hold to sell due to poor same-store-sales growth and EBIT margin, worsened by deteriorating macro conditions and tariffs. DG, typically benefiting from economic downturns, faces intensified demand and margin pressure from tariffs and constrained consumer budgets."

Conduent (NASDAQ:CNDT). Buy to hold by Robert F. Abbott. "Despite positive signs, including institutional interest, I now rate CNDT a Hold due to uncertainty about market recognition and external economic factors."

data.title: OpenAI said to be working on building a social network: report
data.description:
OpenAI, the generative artificial intelligence startup backed by Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) and others, is working on building a social network, The Verge reported.

The project is still in the early stages, but an internal prototype includes a social feed that is focused on ChatGPT’s image generation, the news outlet added, citing sources familiar with the matter.

It's unclear if OpenAI would release the social network as a separate app or as part of ChatGPT, which is used by roughly 10% of the planet, Chief Executive Sam Altman said recently.

OpenAI did not immediately respond to a request for comment.

The news comes as the feud between Altman and Elon Musk, who previously co-founded OpenAI but is now suing the company (along with Microsoft and others), has heated up. In February, a Musk-led group made an all-cash offer for OpenAI to the tune of $97.4B, which Altman subsequently denied. Following the initial report of the offer, Altman appeared to respond and said, "[N]o thank you, but we will buy twitter [sic] for $9.74 billion if you want."

Twitter, now known as X, was recently acquired in an all-stock deal by Musk's OpenAI competitor, xAI, last month.

The Grok chatbot, which recently launched a standalone app, is integrated with X and uses posts to help train its AI models.

data.title: Bets against utility stocks slightly decline in March; AEP most shorted
data.description:
Bets against Utilities Select Sector SPDR Fund ETF (NYSEARCA:XLU) marginally declined in March compared to the end of February, with American Electric Power (AEP) being the most shorted stock in the sector and Sempra (SRE) taking the spot for the least shorted.

Average short interest across S&P 500 utilities stocks was 2.29% of shares float at the end of March, slightly down from 2.31% at the end of February.

The S&P 500’s Utilities Select Sector SPDR Fund ETF (NYSEARCA:XLU) was up 2.76% so far this year, outperforming the broader S&P500 market, which declined over 8% during the same period.

Stocks with the least and largest short positions

Average short interest as a percentage of floating shares

American Electric Power (AEP) had 24.6 million shares sold short as of March 31, or 4.62% of shares float.

CenterPoint Energy (CNP) was the second-most shorted stock with short interest of 4.14% followed by Evergy (EVRG) with 4.09%, AES (AES) with 3.98% and WEC Energy Group (WEC) with 3.85%.

Sempra (SRE) was the least shorted stock, with 7.73 million shares sold short or 1.22% of the shares float, followed by Xcel Energy (XEL) and Public Service Enterprise Group (PEG) with short interest of 1.48% and 1.49% respectively.

Industry analysis

Average short interest as a percentage of floating shares

Independent Power & Renewable Electricity Producers was the most shorted industry within the sector with 3.45% short interest as of March end, compared to 4.40% a month ago.

Gas Utilities stood at the second spot with 3.08% short interest as of the end of March, which decreased from 3.20% at the end of February.

Water Utilities stocks took the third spot with 2.36% short interest as of March end, compared to 2.62% at the end of February.

Bets against the Electric Utilities sector as a whole have increased to 2.17% at the end of March, compared to 2.01% a month ago.

data.title: Ironwood downgraded at Jefferies on apraglutide-related challenges
data.description:
• Ironwood Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:IRWD) has been downgraded to Hold from Buy at Jefferies after the company announced pressures related to its lead asset, apraglutide. 
  • The company announced Monday that it has decided to evaluate strategic alternatives after the FDA required an additional Phase 3 trial to secure approval for its apraglutide.
  • The downgrade by Jefferies reflects increased uncertainty around the drug’s development timeline and approval prospects, with analysts saying that while they previously viewed apraglutide’s clinical profile as a strength, the need for another pivotal study introduces a delay in potential approval, now expected no earlier than 2029, and adds regulatory and financial uncertainty. 
  • Jefferies has lowered its PT to $0.70 from $8 per share.

data.title: Chinese developer Xinyuan said to face involuntary bankruptcy as creditors demand payment
data.description:
Xinyuan Real Estate's (NYSE:XIN) creditors recently filed for involuntary Chapter 11 bankruptcy petition in New York to recover $65.8M in unpaid bonds, making it one of many Chinese builders grappling with financial turmoil in recent years, according to a Tuesday media report that cited a court filing.

Under U.S. bankruptcy law, creditors can request court oversight if a company misses debt payments, allowing the firm to either contest the petition or enter bankruptcy to reorganize.

The company could use its U.S.-based assets to help repay the bonds, the bondholders said in the bankruptcy filing, Bloomberg reported. 

XIN stock traded 14% lower in Tuesday midday trading in the U.S., changing hands at $2.24 per share.

Xinyuan (NYSE:XIN) missed an interest payment in 2022, followed by a failed $170M bond payment that came due in 2024, the filing also showed.

The petition was filed Monday evening at the U.S. Bankruptcy Court for the Southern District of New York.
